subject: DevOps
title: GitHub's MySQL Split-Brain (2018)
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on GitHub's MySQL Split-Brain (2018).

hook.text: October 2018. GitHub was degraded for 24 hours. A routine network change triggered MySQL chaos.

buildup.text: A brief network partition caused the orchestrator to promote a replica to primary.

discovery.text: Two databases thought they were primary. Writes went to both. Data diverged.

twist.text: GitHub chose data integrity over speed. They stayed degraded while reconciling all data.

climax.text: 24 hours of degradation but zero data lost. The postmortem was praised for transparency.

punchline.text: Automated failover can split-brain. Test for partitions.

quiz.question: How did GitHub handle the split-brain situation?
options: They prioritized data integrity over quick recovery
They rolled back all changes from both databases
